repo_purpose: This project exemplifies high-quality enterprise software through a customizable version of the FizzBuzz game, demonstrating architectural principles.
architecture_summary: The architecture revolves around modular design principles with a focus on separation of concerns and clear interface definitions. The core logic of the FizzBuzz game is defined in the `FizzBuzz.java` interface, which allows for flexible implementations. The project utilizes strategy and visitor patterns, particularly in the output generation modules, `OutputGenerationContext` and `OutputGenerationContextVisitor`. The project also incorporates testing with JUnit for validating the FizzBuzz logic, but there is a noted lack of test coverage for other modules and interfaces. The `gradle-wrapper.properties` file ensures consistent build environments through the use of the Gradle wrapper. Furthermore, the `spring.xml` file under `assets/configuration/spring` manages the application's component scanning and dependency injection, indicating the use of the Spring framework.

modules.name: Core FizzBuzz Logic
modules.responsibility: Defines the interface for FizzBuzz logic.

modules.name: Output Generation
modules.responsibility: Handles output generation strategies using visitor patterns.

modules.name: Unit Tests
modules.responsibility: Contains unit tests for testing the FizzBuzz logic.

modules.name: Spring Configuration
modules.responsibility: Handles Spring's component scanning and dependency injection setup.

risks.description: Tests are only for FizzBuzz and do not cover other modules.
No assertion checks on the output of all FizzBuzz calls.
Missing unit tests for the interfaces.
No documentation comments for methods in OutputGenerationContext and OutputGenerationContextVisitor.
Test constants could be better organized or grouped.
[unreadable] indication present in sub-reports, data may be incomplete.
